underlying: Germany ZEW Indicator of Economic Sentiment for May 2026 (Trading Economics / ZEW publication, expected release 2026-05-12)
current_value_source: April 2026 ZEW print released 2026-04-21: -17.2 (down 16.7 pts from March's -0.5). Source: tradingeconomics.com/germany/zew-economic-sentiment-index. Lowest level since Dec 2022; ZEW President Wambach attributed to escalating Iran/Middle East war + long-term energy shortage fears.
method: Normal model: ZEW(May) ~ N(mu=-16, sigma=14). Anchor: April 2026 = -17.2 (latest print, released 2026-04-21). Mu picked at -16 (mild +1.2 mean-reversion bounce off the 16.7-pt April shock; shock moves of this size historically partially retrace within 1-2 months unless catalyst worsens; offset by the fact that the May survey window includes the ECB May rate decision and ongoing Iran/Strait of Hormuz risk, which could push lower). Sigma picked at 14: trailing 24m std of |ZEW(m)-ZEW(m-1)| is 16.9 and trailing 12m is 20.1, but those are inflated by two consecutive shock months (March -58.8 from +58.3, April -16.7). Pre-shock 2024-2025 month-on-month std was closer to 8-12. Compromise at 14 captures normal survey noise plus elevated macro tail (Iran war, ECB meeting inside survey window, energy price). P(YES at strike K) = 1 - Phi((K - mu)/sigma) with mu=-16, sigma=14. Z-scores and probabilities: K=-7.5: z=(-7.5-(-16))/14=0.607, P=1-Phi(0.607)=0.272; K=-10: z=0.429, P=0.334; K=-12.5: z=0.250, P=0.401; K=-15: z=0.071, P=0.472; K=-17.5: z=-0.107, P=0.543; K=-20: z=-0.286, P=0.612; K=-22.5: z=-0.464, P=0.679; K=-25: z=-0.643, P=0.740; K=-27.5: z=-0.821, P=0.794; K=-30: z=-1.000, P=0.841; K=-32.5: z=-1.179, P=0.881; K=-35: z=-1.357, P=0.913; K=-37.5: z=-1.536, P=0.938; K=-40: z=-1.714, P=0.957; K=-42.5: z=-1.893, P=0.971. Floors 0.005, ceiling 0.995 (none triggered).

Key observation: market is essentially a brand-new listing (created 2026-04-27, $0 volume so far) so mid is probably MM-set. Market is centered tighter and lower than my fair (market expects ZEW more like -17 to -19; I have -16). The market clearly disbelieves any meaningful bounce off April's -17.2 anchor, while my model bakes in a mild reversion. Edge is largest on T-7.5 / T-10 / T-12.5 (model says NO is overpriced vs market) and at T-32.5 (market overestimates deep-NO).

Key risks driving confidence to medium-low:
  1) Resolution depends on a single survey (350 financial analysts) released 2026-05-12; survey window is the first week of May. Any news in that narrow window dominates.
  2) Iran/Middle East war: if the conflict escalates further (Strait of Hormuz disruption, oil > $120), May print could easily fall another 10-15 pts. If a ceasefire materializes, the print could rebound 15-20 pts. This is a fat-tailed bimodal risk that a Gaussian understates.
  3) ECB May rate decision falls inside the survey window. A surprise cut would help sentiment; a hold-with-hawkish-tone would hurt.
  4) German fiscal/Bundestag stimulus moves can swing the print.
  5) ZEW shocks of >20 pts have happened twice in the last 3 months (Mar -58.8, Apr -16.7), so empirical sigma is genuinely elevated relative to long-run; my sigma=14 may be too tight if we're in a structurally noisier regime — sensitivity test with sigma=18: T-7.5 P~0.317, T-15 P~0.481, T-25 P~0.691.
  6) Resolution source confirmation: market rules cite 'Germany ZEW economic sentiment index for May 2026'. Trading Economics is the published reference but the underlying release is the ZEW Financial Market Survey from zew.de — both report the same number.
